story.iWant: Swagger documentation automatically converted to Postman Collection
story.soThat: API can be imported into Postman easily
story.tasks: Task 1: Package Installation (AC: 1), Task 2: Service Interface Creation (AC: 2), Task 3: Core Service Implementation (AC: 2, 3, 4), Task 4: Swagger to Postman Conversion Logic (AC: 3, 4), Task 5: Controller Endpoint (AC: 5), Task 6: Download Headers (AC: 6), Task 7: Integration Testing (AC: 3, 5, 6)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. swagger2postman npm package installed - Package added to package.json dependencies, No version conflicts with existing dependencies. 2. Postman converter service created - Service file created at src/common/postman/postman-collection.service.ts, Service implements PostmanCollectionService interface, Dependency injection configured properly. 3. Converter can read OpenAPI 3.0 spec from /api/docs-json - Service fetches Swagger JSON from existing Epic 8 endpoint, Proper error handling for missing/invalid Swagger spec, Validates OpenAPI 3.0 format before conversion. 4. Generate Postman Collection v2.1.0 compatible JSON - Collection structure follows Postman Collection Format v2.1.0, All endpoints from OpenAPI spec included, HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) preserved, Request paths, headers, and parameters converted correctly. 5. Available endpoint: GET /api/docs/postman - Controller endpoint implemented in DocsController, Swagger decorator @ApiOperation added, Proper HTTP response handling. 6. Download header: Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="api-collection.json" - Browser triggers file download automatically, Correct filename format with .json extension, Content-Type: application/json header set.

doc.title: Epic 15: Postman Collection Export
doc.section: Story 15.1: Swagger to Postman Converter Setup
doc.snippet: As a developer, I want Swagger documentation automatically converted to Postman Collection, So that API can be imported into Postman easily. Acceptance Criteria includes swagger2postman npm package installed, Postman converter service created, and Converter can read OpenAPI 3.0 spec from /api/docs-json.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Postman Collection Export
doc.section: Core Components, Swagger to Postman Conversion
doc.snippet: Epic 15 converts Swagger/OpenAPI 3.0 documentation from Epic 8 to generate Postman Collections automatically. Uses swagger2postman NPM package to convert OpenAPI spec to Postman Collection Format v2.1.0.

doc.title: Story 14.2: Enum Integerization
doc.snippet: Previous story completed integer enum conversion with comprehensive testing (e90% coverage). Established patterns for service implementation with interfaces, TypeScript strict typing, and performance considerations to be followed in subsequent stories.

doc.title: PRD - Non-Functional Requirements: Coding Standards
doc.snippet: Standard Service Structure: Services implement interfaces, use constructor dependency injection with private readonly, include comprehensive JSDoc comments, use try-catch blocks for error handling, and follow service/repository pattern with proper separation of concerns.

doc.title: Epic 8: API Documentation - Swagger/OpenAPI
doc.snippet: Epic 8 established Swagger/OpenAPI 3.0 infrastructure. SwaggerModule configured in main.ts at /api/docs endpoint. Swagger JSON available at /api/docs-json for API consumers.

file.reason: Swagger configuration and initialization - SwaggerModule.createDocument() creates OpenAPI spec, setup at /api/docs path, conditional enable via SWAGGER_ENABLED env var
Swagger response type factories used throughout controllers for generating proper OpenAPI documentation with typed responses
Custom Swagger decorators for standardizing API documentation across controllers
CRUD-specific Swagger decorators for standardizing REST endpoint documentation
Example controller with comprehensive Swagger decorators (@ApiTags, @ApiOperation, @ApiOkResponse, @ApiBearerAuth) - reference for Postman converter integration
Example controller showing Swagger decorator patterns for user management endpoints - referenced for Postman collection generation

npm.name: swagger-ui-express
npm.description: Swagger UI middleware for Express-based NestJS apps

npm.name: swagger2postman
npm.description: Package to convert Swagger/OpenAPI specs to Postman Collection format

story-context.constraints: - Must use swagger2postman NPM package for conversion (Epic 15 requirement) - Follow NestJS service pattern with interfaces and dependency injection (NFR-4.8) - Swagger JSON source: /api/docs-json endpoint from Epic 8 (dependency) - Postman Collection Format v2.1.0 compliance required - All endpoints from OpenAPI spec must be included in collection - Service location: src/common/postman/ (following common module pattern) - Controller integration: Extend existing DocsController - Error handling: Proper try-catch with specific exceptions (NFR-4.11) - Testing: e90% coverage required (Epic 14-2 standard) - Type safety: TypeScript strict typing for all collection structures

interface.name: IPostmanCollectionService
PostmanCollection

tests.standards: Unit tests using NestJS Testing Module with jest. Service tests follow Arrange-Act-Assert pattern with comprehensive mocking. Test coverage target: e90% for service methods. Integration tests verify full endpoint flow from request to response. All tests must pass before story completion. Manual testing includes importing generated Postman Collection into Postman application to verify correctness.
tests.locations: Unit tests: src/common/postman/__tests__/ (per NFR-4.12 pattern) Integration tests: test/integration/docs/ directory Test files follow pattern: *.spec.ts
tests.ideas: Test AC1: Verify swagger2postman package installation and no dependency conflicts Test AC2: Verify PostmanCollectionService interface implementation and DI configuration Test AC3: Verify OpenAPI spec fetching from /api/docs-json with error handling for invalid specs Test AC4: Verify Postman Collection v2.1.0 structure with all endpoints and HTTP methods preserved Test AC5: Verify GET /api/docs/postman endpoint returns 200 with valid JSON Test AC6: Verify download headers (Content-Disposition, Content-Type) trigger file download Test AC7: Verify collection imports successfully into Postman application
